title: Wire reconcile('post-authoring') into the quarters file.* actions (closes the unwired reconcile gap)

tests.summary: 3 passed — file.create/file.update fire reconcile({trigger:'post-authoring', surfaces:[touched]}) exactly once, scoped to the touched surface; a failed action (duplicate create) does NOT fire reconcile. Full suite 506 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed.

build.summary: Next build compiled, all routes rendered

e2e.summary: N/A — lib dispatch slice; proof is the committed vitest gate

notes: reconcile() previously had NO production caller (its post-authoring/on-demand triggers were aspirational). Now runQuartersAction awaits reconcile({trigger:'post-authoring', surfaces:[req.surface]}) after a SUCCESSFUL file.create/update/delete, so a freshly-authored loose skill/command/rule is captured into the Seed store and becomes promotable to owned. Best-effort (try/catch + warn) so a reconcile failure never fails the authoring action; scoped to the touched surface so it doesn't rescan everything. Sandbox-safe: capturedFittingsDir() honors GARRISON_HOME and the only existing file.* test (primitive-files.test.ts) already sandboxes it; the new test mocks reconcile (spread over the real module). Satisfies brief §3 token reconcile-wired-ok. NOTE: the Improver (MR5) will rely on this so its hosted-API file.* applies reconcile automatically.
